channel.title: Inference — AI Newsletter
channel.description: The signal in the noise. Curated AI trends, tools, and research — updated every Monday.

item.title: Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to ban Anthropic from US government contracts following the ongoing Pentagon dispute over Claude's military use.
item.author: Will Knight, WIRED.com

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: A Dev.to walkthrough covering Boost implementation patterns — practical guide for teams already using the library.
item.author: Omar Corredor Pacheco

item.title: CoPaw
item.description: CoPaw is a self-hosted AI assistant with multi-platform chat integrations and local or cloud deployment.
item.author: agentscope-ai

item.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
item.description: IssueScout surfaces good-first-issue labels across repos, enriched with community health scores and AI difficulty estimates for new contributors.
item.author: Hacker News (AI)

item.title: Letting Machines Decide What Matters
item.description: Physicists are using ML to sift through Large Hadron Collider data for anomalies outside the Standard Model, potentially identifying undiscovered particles.
item.author: Eliza Strickland

item.title: Google AI Introduces STATIC: A Sparse Matrix Framework Delivering 948x Faster Constrained Decoding for LLM Based Generative Retrieval
item.description: STATIC going open lowers the bar for experimentation and custom deployments (via MarkTechPost).
item.author: Asif Razzaq

item.title: OpenAI and Amazon announce strategic partnership
item.description: The OpenAI announcement carries implications for budgets and vendor relationships (via OpenAI Blog).
item.author: OpenAI Blog

item.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
item.description: OpenAI disclosed further details about its Pentagon deal, with CEO Altman acknowledging the agreement was rushed and the optics aren't great.
item.author: Anthony Ha

item.title: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
item.description: Lenovo showed desktop AI concepts at MWC including a robot arm companion and an AI-powered desktop clock hub.
item.author: Andrew Liszewski

item.title: The Data Centers Have Arrived at the Edge of the Arctic Circle
item.description: AI compute demand is pushing data centre expansion to the Arctic Circle, where cold climates cut cooling costs but infrastructure challenges remain.
item.author: Joel Khalili

item.title: FireRedTeam Releases FireRed-OCR-2B Utilizing GRPO to Solve Structural Hallucinations in Tables and LaTeX for Software Developers
item.description: FireRed-OCR-2B is a 2B-parameter OCR model trained with GRPO to fix structural hallucinations in table and LaTeX recognition.
item.author: Asif Razzaq

item.title: Firefox 149 beta develops a split personality
item.description: Firefox 149 beta promotes split-view mode (two pages side-by-side) to default-on, adds hardware-accelerated PDF rendering and address autofill.
item.author: Liam Proven

item.title: Iran all but vanishes from the global internet amid US-Israel strikes
item.description: Quick update on Iran — relevant if you're evaluating tools in this space (via The Register).
item.author: Carly Page

item.title: Microsoft's Project Silica promises eternal storage. It can't get there from here
item.description: Project Silica uses femtosecond lasers to write 2 TB across hundreds of layers in glass plates readable for 10,000 years, but 20 Mbps write speeds make commercialisation unlikely.
item.author: Rupert Goodwins

item.title: Flaw-Finding AI Assistants Face Criticism for Speed, Accuracy
item.description: AI-powered vulnerability scanners are drawing criticism for high false-positive rates and slow scan times compared to traditional SAST/DAST tools.
item.author: Robert Lemos

item.title: Podcast: [Video Podcast] Frictionless DevEx with Nicole Forsgren
item.description: In this episode, Thomas Betts talks with Dr. Nicole Forsgren about frictionless developer experience, measuring DevEx, and how teams can reduce toil in their workflows.
item.author: Nicole Forsgren
